title: Facing a lot of timeout issue with custom client
response:
Subject: MCP Server SSE Request Timeout Due to Initialization Delay and Default Timeout Limit  
Summary: The custom SSE client experienced request timeouts because the MCP server's internal session initialization was not complete before requests were sent, causing "Received request before initialization was complete" errors and eventual SSE response timeouts. The issue was temporarily mitigated by adding a long client-side delay after initialization, but the root cause is the MCP server's default 60-second timeout and initialization handling, which the maintainer plans to address by offloading crawling to a separate process in future versions.

title: vdb6 full database creation requires 32GB machine
response:
Subject: High memory usage and crash during SQLite index creation due to in-memory temp storage

Summary: The full database creation process crashes on machines with less than 32GB RAM because SQLite's temporary files and indices are stored in memory, causing excessive memory consumption during VACUUM and index creation. Changing SQLite's temp_store setting from memory to file storage mitigates the issue, allowing the process to run on lower-memory machines.

title: [BUG] CodeAgent no longer takes system prompt (?) - Breaking Change
response:
Subject: Breaking Change in CodeAgent System Prompt Handling and Documentation Mismatch

Summary: The issue arose because the CodeAgent class in the smolagents library changed how system prompts are specified—from a direct system_prompt parameter to using a prompt_templates dictionary—causing older scripts to break. The confusion was compounded by outdated documentation references to the legacy transformers library instead of the current smolagents docs, and the resolution involved clarifying the new usage pattern and pointing users to the updated documentation.

title: [BUG] Output is missing newlines which makes copying to clipboard suboptimal
response:
Subject: Terminal output formatting lacks explicit newlines, causing clipboard copy issues  
Summary: The issue arises because the smolagents output formatting relies on the Rich library's terminal wrapping instead of inserting actual newline characters, resulting in mangled text when copied from the terminal to a text editor. This behavior is by design in Rich, and the recommended workaround is to redirect output to a file or access the agent's memory for structured output rather than expecting clipboard-friendly formatting.

title: [BUG] Groq API incompatible with smolagents system messages when using OpenAI endpoint via LiteLLMModel
response:
Subject: Groq API message format incompatibility causing OpenAI 400 BadRequestError in smolagents LiteLLMModel

Summary: The issue was caused by Groq's OpenAI-compatible API requiring system message content to be a string, but smolagents' LiteLLMModel sending system messages in an incompatible format. The problem was resolved by enabling message flattening (flatten_messages_as_text) for Groq models in smolagents, ensuring system messages are sent as plain strings, which fixed the 400 invalid_request_error.

title: [Bug]: Target Closed Error: BrowserType.launch: Target page, context or browser has been closed
response:
Subject: Target Closed Error due to improper async browser lifecycle management in crawl4ai usage

Summary: The issue occurs because the script launches and closes the browser context within each async run call, but uses asyncio.run repeatedly in a synchronous loop, causing the browser or page to be closed prematurely. Proper management of the async event loop and browser lifecycle is needed to avoid the "Target page, context or browser has been closed" error when scraping multiple pages.

title: [BUG]: `PhoenixTraceProvider` does not pass span attributes to custom sampler's `should_sample(...)` as done with  OpenTelemetry’s standard `TracerProvider`
response:
Subject: Missing span attributes in custom sampler's should_sample due to OpenTelemetry design limitation  
Summary: The issue was that PhoenixTraceProvider's custom sampler did not receive span attributes in should_sample(), unlike expected from standard OpenTelemetry behavior. This is not a bug in Phoenix but a fundamental OpenTelemetry design constraint where sampling decisions occur before span attributes are set, so attributes passed to should_sample() are always None unless provided at span creation time.
